chapter_title.ar: باب الْمُدَالَسَةِ فِي النِّكَاحِ وَ مَا تُرَدُّ مِنْهُ الْمَرْأَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 66 – The deceptive woman in the marriage and what the woman can be rejected from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/152)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/152)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: تُرَدُّ الْبَرْصَاءُ وَ الْمَجْنُونَةُ وَ الْمَجْذُومَةُ قُلْتُ الْعَوْرَاءُ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziiyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Abu Jameela, from Zayd Al Shahaam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘She can be returned, the woman with vitiligo, and the insanity, and the leprosy’. I said, ‘The blind?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from abu Jamilah from Zayd al-Shahham who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Marriage of a woman with illness of al-Bars (albino), insanity and leprosy is revoked and invalid.’ I asked about a blind. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it does not apply to her.’”
